### class `Abs`

Absolute value, `|child|`.

**Members**

- `child: Base`
- `toJs: () => string` ‚Äî Serialise to a JavaScript expression string.
- `toPy: (displayNames: Map<string, string>) => string` ‚Äî Serialise to a Python/NumPy expression; `displayNames` maps internal symbol names to their Python identifiers.

- `getSymbols: (symbols: Set<string>) => Set<string>` ‚Äî Collect every variable/parameter symbol referenced in this subtree into `symbols`.
- `id: number` ‚Äî Process-unique identifier, assigned on construction.

### class `Base`

Common ancestor for every expression-tree node.

Subclasses implement the serialisers and tree operations below. Each instance
gets a process-unique {@link Base.id} on construction, used to target nodes in
{@link Base.replace}.

**Members**

- `id: number` ‚Äî Process-unique identifier, assigned on construction.
- `toJs: () => string` ‚Äî Serialise to a JavaScript expression string.
- `toPy: (displayNames: Map<string, string>) => string` ‚Äî Serialise to a Python/NumPy expression; `displayNames` maps internal symbol names to their Python identifiers.
- `toTex: (texNames: Map<string, string>) => string` ‚Äî Serialise to a LaTeX expression; `texNames` maps internal symbol names to their LaTeX rendering.
- `toSBML: () => string` ‚Äî Serialise to an SBML MathML fragment.
- `toWat: (ctx: WatContext) => string` ‚Äî Serialise to a WebAssembly Text (WAT) expression, using `ctx` to resolve variable/parameter memory layout.
- `getSymbols: (symbols: Set<string>) => Set<string>` ‚Äî Collect every variable/parameter symbol referenced in this subtree into `symbols`.
- `replace: (id: number, next: Base) => { node: Base; changed: boolean; }` ‚Äî Return a copy of this subtree with the node whose id is `id` replaced by
